channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Grassley rips Wray's 'failed' leadership at FBI with 11 pages of examples in blistering 'no confidence' letter
item.description: Longtime Republican Sen. Chuck Grassley slammed FBI chiief Christopher Wray for "failed" leadership in a lengthy letter where he expressed "no confidence" in the director.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Both sides in abortion debate seek clues in Trump's nominees to key positions
item.description: As Donald Trump’s Cabinet begins to take shape, the nominees offer a preview of how his administration might handle abortion after he repeatedly flip-flopped on the issue on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Military pauses Osprey flights again after more metal failures are found in near crash in November
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon is temporarily pausing flights again of its fleet of V-22 Ospreys after weakened metal components possibly played a role in another near crash, the latest setback for an embattled aircraft whose safety problems have grown.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear picked to lead Democratic efforts to win governorships in 2026 midterms
item.description: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear is taking on a lead role for Democratic efforts to win governorships in the 2026 midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Blue state attorney general advises state officials on how to resist ‘draconian’ Trump deportations
item.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta has joined a growing list of Democratic leaders vowing to resist President-elect Donald Trump and border czar Tom Homan’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal from Boston parents over race bias in elite high school admissions
item.description: The Supreme Court has rejected an appeal from Boston parents who claimed a temporary admissions policy for the city’s elite high schools discriminated against white students and those of Asian descent.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump return to DC looms over Kennedy Center Honors
item.description: In a night filled with political moments — including multiple nods to President Joe Biden, a Grateful Dead tribute from former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Wisconsin parents' challenge to school guidance for transgender students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday rejected an appeal from Wisconsin parents who wanted to challenge a school district's guidance for supporting transgender students. The justices, acting in a case from Eau Claire, left in place an appellate ruling dismissing the parents' lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic governors appoint incoming California, New Jersey senators 3 weeks early
item.description: Senator-elects Andy Kim, D-N.J., and Adam Schiff, D-Calif., will be sworn into the Senate about three weeks early on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lara Trump announces she is stepping down as RNC co-chair amidst talk she may be up for Florida Senate seat
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) citing that during her time the RNC had three distinct goals, and they had all been accomplished.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump names Alina Habba as counselor to the president; reveals several State Department picks
item.description: President-elect Trump announced several nominations and appointments on Sunday, including his attorney Alina Habba, who will serve as counselor to the president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fall of Assad regime a 'moment of historic opportunity' for Syrian people, Biden says
item.description: President Biden said the U.S. will support Syria's neighboring nations and help bolster stability in the region after Syrian dictator Bashar al-Assad fled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: Trump spoke with "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker, and the two discussed Trump's plans for his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Assad arrives in Moscow, is granted asylum by Russia
item.description: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad has arrived in Moscow and was granted asylum by the Russian government, a longtime ally of the Syrian leader in the nearly 14-year-long civil war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump focuses on tariffs, not retribution, in first network TV interview
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump lays out plan for his first 100 days during interview with NBC News' "Meet the Press.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP to choose chairs for influential committees: Who’s in the running
item.description: House Republican leaders will choose new chairs for a number of influential committees this week, people who will have the power to help deliver on President-elect Trump’s priorities in the GOP trifecta over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats say they'll avoid election challenges on Jan. 6
item.description: House Democrats say they’ll skip the election protests they’ve staged on Jan. 6 in past presidential cycles, four years after supporters of President-elect Trump stormed the Capitol in an attempt to interrupt the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says US should stay out of fighting in Syria as opposition forces gain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. military should stay out of the fast-escalating conflict in Syria, where a dramatic rebel offensive reached the capital and threatened the rule of Syria's Russian- and Iranian-allied president.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, WILL WEISSERT and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clintons urge voters agitated by today's politics to remain involved in public service
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Voters dejected by the presidential election results need to find a way to give back and remain involved, Bill and Hillary Rodham Clinton said Saturday as they celebrated the 20th anniversary of the Clinton presidential library.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump isn't back in office but he's already pushing his agenda and negotiating with world leaders
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — He's making threats, traveling abroad and negotiating with world leaders. Donald Trump has more than a month and a half to go before he's sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Election Day has long passed. In some states, legislatures are working to undermine the results
item.description: Voters in some parts of the country are discovering that having their say at the ballot box is not necessarily the final word, even though this year's election was over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Montana lawmakers cross the aisle to block a trans bathroom ban in the state capitol
item.description: Some Republicans joined Democrats in rejecting the measure, which would have restricted bathroom access. Rep. Zooey Zephyr, Montana's first openly transgender legislator, won a second term last month.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Condo deadline looms: Owners must confront years of neglect
item.description: The condo conundrum continues as the December 31st inspection deadline is only a few weeks away. State lawmakers promise action on the issue next session, but the clock is ticking.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The fight against medical debt is pivoting to the states after Trump's election
item.description: Patient and consumer advocates fear a new Trump administration will scale back federal efforts to expand financial protections for patients and shield them from debt.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey, MPR News

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump falls just below 50% in popular vote, but gets more than in past elections
item.description: This year's popular-vote margin is the second-closest since 1968 and still tightening. It shows just how closely divided the country is politically, and that any shift to the right is marginal.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News
